incidents.victim: 200+ organizations worldwide (government agencies, law enforcement, email providers)
incidents.vector: DNS hijacking of 18,000+ SOHO routers (Mikrotik, TP-Link) to intercept Microsoft Office OAuth tokens without deploying malware.
incidents.impact: Authentication tokens stolen from 5,000+ devices across 200+ organizations, bypassing multi-factor authentication. Attackers gained direct access to victim email accounts and sensitive communications.

incidents.victim: U.S. water, energy, and municipal government systems
incidents.vector: Exploitation of internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices, specifically Rockwell Automation PLCs, using CVE-2021-22681.
incidents.impact: Operational disruptions and financial losses across multiple U.S. critical infrastructure sectors. Attackers manipulated PLC project files, HMI and SCADA displays. Activity escalated in response to the U.S.-Iran military conflict.

incidents.vector: Spear-phishing emails with malicious archives delivering LucidRook, a Lua-based stager with Rust-compiled components, via DLL sideloading and living-off-the-land techniques.
incidents.impact: Sophisticated malware deployed to target organizations involved in Taiwan's government and academic sectors. LucidRook provides persistent access with C2 via compromised FTP servers, enabling long-term intelligence collection.

incidents.victim: Fortinet FortiClient EMS customers (~2,000 exposed instances)
incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS 9.8) in FortiClient EMS, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation observed since March 31, with attacks ramping up after April 6 hotfix disclosure. This is the 10th Fortinet vulnerability added to CISA's KEV catalog since early 2025. Full patch not yet available.

incidents.victim: 300+ organizations across Israel, UAE, US, UK, Europe, Saudi Arabia
incidents.vector: Large-scale password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments. Targeting correlated with cities struck by Iranian missiles, suggesting coordination with kinetic operations.
incidents.impact: Credential compromise attempts across critical infrastructure sectors in multiple allied nations. Campaign appears designed to support bombing damage assessment and kinetic military operations.

incidents.vector: Phishing attack that compromised access to a limited number of client files. Data posted online by Silent Ransom Group on March 30.
incidents.impact: Client materials for 10 clients accessed and leaked. Jones Day is a major U.S. firm known for representing President Trump and large corporations. Federal Circuit IP cases may be among exposed files.

incidents.vector: Social engineering of lead maintainer via fake company with cloned identities, fake Slack workspace, and malicious file during a Microsoft Teams call. Malware gave full remote access, bypassing 2FA.
incidents.impact: Two malicious Axios versions published to NPM with embedded RAT, estimated ~600,000 downloads in ~3 hours. Credentials scraped from infected systems. Google attributed the attack to North Korean group UNC1069.

incidents.victim: Multiple organizations (Trivy, Checkmarx, LiteLLM, Telnyx, and downstream victims)
incidents.vector: Supply chain attack originating from Trivy vulnerability scanner compromise (CVE-2026-33634), spreading via CanisterWorm across GitHub Actions, PyPI, npm, Docker Hub, and OpenVSX.
incidents.impact: Approximately 300 GB of credentials stolen from CI/CD pipelines. Now in monetization phase with dual ransomware tracks (CipherForce and Vect). Databricks investigating alleged downstream compromise. CISA KEV remediation deadline is April 8.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed a wiper payload via their existing CanisterWorm infrastructure, targeting systems with Iran's timezone or Farsi locale settings.
incidents.impact: If the wiper detects an Iranian system with Kubernetes cluster access, it destroys data on every node. Otherwise it wipes the local machine. Represents an unusual pivot from financial crime to politically motivated destruction.

incidents.vector: Months-long social engineering campaign culminating in malicious Apple TestFlight app and VS Code extensions deployed on employee devices after building trust through in-person conference meetings.
incidents.impact: Attackers multi-signed transactions that emptied Drift's treasury of $280 million. Investigation linked the attack to a North Korean group known for cryptocurrency theft.

incidents.vector: Wiper attack attributed to Iran-linked threat actors in the context of the U.S.-Iran military conflict.
incidents.impact: 200,000 company devices wiped in one of the most disruptive Iranian cyber operations against a U.S. company. Stryker has since reported it is back up and operational.

incidents.vector: Claimed breach of personal email account, framed as retaliation for FBI domain seizure targeting the group.
incidents.impact: Travel details, correspondence, Global Entry and loyalty account numbers published online. Claim not independently verified but represents escalation of Iranian hack-and-leak operations targeting senior U.S. law enforcement.

incidents.vector: Breach of the European Commission's Amazon Web Services account on March 24, detected and blocked by security teams.
incidents.impact: Internal data may have been stolen before the breach was contained. An internal investigation is ongoing to determine the full extent. The threat actor indicated plans to leak stolen data online.

incidents.vector: Pay2Key ransomware deployed using compromised administrative credentials and living-off-the-land techniques.
incidents.impact: Assessed as state-aligned disruption rather than financially motivated — deliberate targeting of U.S. healthcare during the Iran conflict. Represents revival of the Pay2Key ransomware operation.

incidents.vector: Data theft and extortion attempt by LAPSUS$ group. After failing to find buyers, the 3 GB archive was released for free.
incidents.impact: Partially verified dump contains GitHub user information for internal developers, employee data across clinical research subsidiaries, and internal source code structures. AstraZeneca has not issued a public statement after 96+ hours.

incidents.vector: AI-assisted automation to exploit GitHub pull_request_target workflow misconfiguration, opening 475+ malicious pull requests in 26 hours to steal credentials.
incidents.impact: Fewer than 10% of attempts succeeded, but at least two NPM packages were compromised. Demonstrates how AI has lowered the barrier for large-scale supply chain attacks.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2025-53521, an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager.
incidents.impact: Critical vulnerability being exploited in the wild across enterprise networks. CISA added to KEV catalog; NCSC issued urgent advisory recommending isolation and rebuild of affected systems.

incidents.vector: Qilin ransomware deployed against the party's IT infrastructure, forcing a full network shutdown.
incidents.impact: Employee records stolen; the party says no member data was compromised. Qilin is the most prolific ransomware group worldwide in 2025-2026 with 200+ victims per month.

incidents.victim: Government officials and high-risk individuals globally
incidents.vector: Targeted attacks against messaging app accounts (WhatsApp, Signal, Messenger) using social engineering, phishing links, QR codes, and device-linking abuse.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued joint advisory warning of growing Russian exploitation of messaging apps to compromise accounts of government officials and individuals with access to sensitive information.

incidents.vector: Large-scale automated credential harvesting exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in Next.js applications using the NEXUS Listener framework.
incidents.impact: Attackers automatically extract cloud tokens, database credentials, and SSH keys from hundreds of compromised hosts. Creates significant risk for lateral movement and supply chain integrity.
